decisions.summary: Detection signal reuses the existing CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_PATTERNS tmux-tail match already proven in deacon.ts checkApiErrorAgents(), extracted into a single shared predicate. We do NOT use the heavier server-side percentUsed for the wedge classification — that reads the session JSONL per agent and is reserved for the proactive (pre-ceiling) trigger only.

The persisted context-saturation marker lives on the agent's runtime.json (a new contextSaturatedAt field), set on the FIRST detection in the patrol and cleared as soon as the tail no longer shows overflow. runtime.json is chosen because the dashboard /api/health/agents route (determineHealthStatus) already reads it cheaply and it is keyed by agentId. This is the single source of truth the classifiers consume; it is independent of the review-status `stuck` flag, which is only set after recovery gives up.

Recovery escalation ladder for a context-overflow incident: (1) /compact (existing — works only while still UNDER the hard ceiling); (2) if /compact does not clear the overflow after the settle window, send /clear followed by a reseed nudge that instructs the agent to reconstruct WIP from continue.json + beads + git working tree — NEVER re-sending the old transcript; (3) if /clear+reseed still does not clear it, markWorkspaceStuck. Root cause the current code misses: /compact must round-trip the full transcript through the model to summarize, so an agent already at the 400 hard ceiling 400s on /compact too — Claude Code's own banner recommends /clear, which frees tokens with no model round-trip.

Both health classifiers — determineHealthStatus (dashboard /api/health/agents) and cloister/health.ts (getAgentHealth + health summary) — must treat a present context-saturation marker as a distinct non-healthy 'wedged' state that OVERRIDES the lastActivity-based 'healthy'. The misreport root cause is that both classifiers are purely lastActivity-time based, and a wedged agent re-renders its 400 error / gets poked, keeping lastActivity recent.

Proactive compaction is in scope (issue Expected #3). It is split into a verification bead (determine whether CLIProxy/gpt-5.5 sessions auto-compact and what ceiling the proxy enforces) that INFORMS a high-water-mark trigger bead (send /compact when server-side percentUsed crosses ~85% while the agent is idle and still under the ceiling, cooldown-guarded). Ordered AFTER detection+recovery so the safety net lands first.

AUTO-MODE: gpt-5.5 is treated as a preferred, well-performing work model per the issue and project memory — this is a substrate context-lifecycle gap, NOT a reason to avoid or special-case gpt-5.5. No model is blocklisted; the fixes apply uniformly to every CLIProxy-routed and native session.

hazards.summary: /clear drops ALL in-session conversation context. Uncommitted work-in-progress could appear lost.
hazards.mitigation: /clear resets only the conversation, NOT the filesystem — the workspace working tree, continue.json, and beads survive. The reseed nudge must explicitly instruct the agent to read continue.json, run `bd ready`/`bd show`, and inspect `git status`/`git diff` to reconstruct exactly where it was. Use the /clear tier ONLY after /compact has demonstrably failed to clear the overflow (hard-ceiling case), where the alternative is a permanently-dead agent anyway.

hazards.summary: Distinguishing the hard ceiling (must /clear) from a still-recoverable-by-compact overflow. /clear-ing an agent that could have compacted loses more context than necessary.
hazards.mitigation: Always try /compact first (cheap and lossless while under the ceiling); fall back to /clear+reseed only when /compact fails to clear the overflow after the settle window. The existing compact-attempt loop-guard already counts attempts — escalate to /clear instead of straight to stuck.

hazards.summary: False-positive health downgrade: naive tmux-tail matching of 'context window' could fire on an agent that merely printed the phrase.
hazards.mitigation: Keep the existing constraint that the pattern is judged only from the recent tail (last ~40 lines, adjacent to the idle prompt / 'API Error: 400'), matching the proven behavior already in checkApiErrorAgents(). Unit-test the predicate against both true wedges and benign mentions.

hazards.summary: For CLIProxy-routed models the real context ceiling (and whether native auto-compact fires) is unknown — percentUsed against capability.contextWindow may be unreliable, which would make a proactive trigger fire at the wrong threshold.
hazards.mitigation: The proactive-verify bead establishes the actual CLIProxy compaction behavior and a conservative ceiling constant BEFORE the trigger bead consumes it. The trigger keys off observed input tokens vs that conservative ceiling, and is cooldown-guarded so a wrong threshold cannot spam /compact.

hazards.summary: Marker staleness: if a human manually /clears a wedged agent (or it otherwise recovers), the saturation marker must clear or the agent stays misreported as wedged forever.
hazards.mitigation: The deacon detection loop clears contextSaturatedAt on every cycle where the tail no longer shows overflow — the marker is a live mirror of the current tail, not a sticky flag.

hazards.summary: determineHealthStatus is performance-sensitive — a prior fix removed per-agent capturePane from the /api/health/agents poll to stop pinning the dashboard CPU.
hazards.mitigation: The classifier reads only the cheap persisted runtime.json marker (already read for lastActivity) — it MUST NOT introduce a per-agent tmux capture. Only the deacon patrol (already capturing panes) writes the marker.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1615: Substrate: work agents hard-wedge at the model context ceiling (400) and are misreported 'healthy'; PAN-1415 remediation can't recover them
